failures.rung1_note: SB1X_BAIL (SPRING_BOOT_1X_NEEDS_MANUAL), deterministically confirmed (not cluster-assumed). Repo is a WebGoat fork on Spring Boot 1.5.12 / Spring 4.3. REAL regression: baseline GREEN under JDK8 (115 pass), but post-bump JDK11 tests fail with ClassNotFoundException: java.sql.Connection/SQLException via org.springframework.beans.CachedIntrospectionResults -> BeanInfoFactory. Root cause = JDK9+ moved java.sql.* from the bootstrap to the PLATFORM classloader; Spring 4.3 looks them up via a classloader that no longer sees them (Spring-4.x-too-old-for-JDK11). Reproduces on the deterministic happy path. Prescribed fix sb1_to_sb2.sh RUNS (rc=0, SB->2.7.18) but WebGoat's custom code does NOT compile on SB2: references SB2-removed APIs OpenRewrite cannot migrate -- org.thymeleaf.resourceresolver (LessonTemplateResolver/AsciiDoctorTemplateResolver), org.springframework.boot.actuate.endpoint.mvc (Endpoint/PluginEndpointPublisher), org.springframework.boot.context.embedded + EmbeddedServletContainerFactory (WebGoat.java). Needs manual migration. SECONDARY blocker: deterministic bump_8_to_11 also crashes the Java8toJava11 recipe on the :idor lessons module (RECIPE_FAIL_AP) -- orthogonal, also unscripted.
jhipster Angular frontend-coupled build / recipe step fails (or needs Cassandra/Mongo/ES DB)
5 test classes fail at baseline too (Gmail SMTP auth + flaky placeholder assert); bump conserves (bump_rc=0, same fails pre/post)

ENV_FALSEPOS: baseline already non-green (rc_pre=1); verdict keys on rc_post!=0 but rc_pre=1 too. Test conservation HELD: pre_pass=3710 == post_pass=3710, regressed=0 (per trajectory.json). Two non-bump baseline causes: (a) env-sensitive MainTest.testExistingTargetFileButWithoutReadAccess (asserts runtime.exit(-1); sandbox uid can still read the file -> exit 0); (b) checkstyle's own JaCoCo offline-instrumentation exec goal (org.jacoco.agent.rt internal Offline class). Deterministic happy path CLEAN: bump_11_to_17 rc=0 + exact harness build_post (release=17 compile) rc=0; bump raised java.version 11->17 and converted source/target->release. java17_compat correctly left jacoco 0.8.7 untouched (checkstyle property maven.jacoco.plugin.version does not match the jacoco*.version bump pattern); only added the surefire --add-opens. Fork IlCanMert/seng326-checkstyle = identical sha -> identical source/baseline.
ExceptionInInitializer was the add-opens reflection issue; now PASSES (21/21) with java17_compat committed (7431b557)

NICHE_BUILD confirmed = TABLE-COVERED tools.jar removal (agent-application failure). Deterministic bump_8_to_11 (and 11->17) aborts at the Java8toJava11 recipe step: bump_rc=1, baseline green (879) -> post 0. Error: 'dependency com.sun:tools:jar (system) Could not find artifact ... /lib/tools.jar'. google-java-format pins a com.sun:tools system-scoped dependency at ${java.home}/../lib/tools.jar; tools.jar was REMOVED in JDK 9+ (compiler APIs moved into the jdk.compiler module), so Maven cannot resolve it under JDK 11. This is EXACTLY the SKILL failure-table tools.jar row -- which names google-java-format as its example: delete the tools system dependency, and because the project calls com.sun.tools.javac.* internals, add --add-exports jdk.compiler/com.sun.tools.javac.*=ALL-UNNAMED to maven-compiler-plugin compilerArgs + surefire argLine, using source/target (NOT --release, which forbids --add-exports to JDK-internal packages). So it is a known, documented case the rung-2 agent failed to apply (the 'agent does not apply failure-table rows' finding), not a new gap. Remedy not auto-verified here (multi-step). Same root cause for both google-java-format shas (8->11 13f608, 11->17 3c191c).

FIXABLE (verified remedy): old maven-surefire-plugin 2.20.1 NPEs under JDK 9+. Source = spring-boot-starter-parent:2.0.0.RELEASE pins surefire 2.20.1 (known surefire JDK9+ NPE, fixed in 2.22.0). Deterministic repro on the standalone project spring-boot-2-jdbc-with-h2: baseline JDK8 rc=0, bump_8_to_11 rc=0, post JDK11 'mvn test' rc=1 with 'maven-surefire-plugin:2.20.1 ... NullPointerException'. FIX VERIFIED: -Dmaven-surefire-plugin.version=2.22.2 -> post JDK11 rc=0. Conservation already held in the sweep (regressed_test_count=0, rc_pre=0) so the verdict is also an rc_post-heuristic over-report, but the underlying surefire-too-old issue is real and fixable. Candidate harness fix: in java11_compat, when effective surefire is 2.20.x/2.21.x (typically spring-boot-starter-parent < 2.2), add a <maven-surefire-plugin.version>2.22.2</...> property floor. NOTE: surefire is INHERITED here (not pom-pinned), so the existing literal/property bump regex does NOT catch it -- needs SB-parent-version detection + a validation sweep before shipping. Repo in28minutes/spring-boot-examples is a COLLECTION of standalone projects (root has no <module>s). ppashapu/my-personal-repository @ same sha 6db697ee is byte-identical -> same fix.

ASM_SB2_JDK17 (table-covered; agent-application failure). Deterministic bump_11_to_17 repro: bump_rc=0, rc_pre=1, rc_post=1. Baseline is 91% ENV-broken: 222/243 errors (InvalidDataAccessResourceUsage = no DB in sandbox); only 5 env-independent tests pass. Happy path loses exactly 1 of those 5: UserServiceTests.shouldNotFindAuthenticated. Root cause: SB 2.2.4.RELEASE -> Spring Framework 5.2.3, whose bundled ASM 7 cannot read JDK17 bytecode (major 61) during component scanning -> 'BeanDefinitionStoreException: ASM ClassReader failed to parse class file' on AuthoritiesServiceTests.class -> context fails -> the test errors. Passed at JDK11 (major 55, readable by ASM 7). The OTHER agent-reported regression (ValidatorTests.shouldNotValidateWhenFirstNameEmpty) did NOT regress deterministically -> that was Qwen's 41-line SecurityConfiguration.java hand-edit, not the happy path. This signature IS in the SKILL failure table (ASM row -> sb2_to_sb3.sh), so it is a KNOWN, table-covered case the rung-2 agent failed to apply (the 'agent does not apply failure-table rows' finding). PENDING: verify sb2_to_sb3 actually conserves the 5 env-independent tests here (vs a deeper bail like WebGoat).

JHIPSTER_COMPLEX confirmed, root cause CORRECTED (not frontend-coupled). Deterministic bump_8_to_11 fails at the Java8toJava11 recipe step: the recipe compiles the project under JDK11, but the JHipster-8 ANNOTATION-PROCESSING stack has a cascade of JDK11 incompatibilities, peeled one by one: (1) NoClassDefFoundError javax/xml/bind/JAXBException (JAXB removed in 11) -- jaxb on <annotationProcessorPaths> fixes it [java11_compat only adds EE deps to <dependencies>, which annotationProcessorPaths excludes]; (2) NoClassDefFoundError javax/annotation/Generated (MapStruct @Generated; javax.annotation removed) -- annotation-api on the processor path fixes it; (3) Fatal NullPointerException inside the processor itself (old MapStruct/jpamodelgen NPE under JDK11). Baseline green (149). Neither the surefire nor the new Mockito floor is reached (recipe aborts first). Proper remedy is a JHipster/Spring-Boot version upgrade (brings JDK11-safe processors + drops JAXB), out of scope for a one-LTS-step bump -> genuine bail. Likely the same for the H2-based jhipster samples; the Cassandra/Mongo/ES variants additionally need a DB container.

JDK_TIMESTAMP_PRECISION (test-design/env, not migration-fixable). Only 2 tests regress: CustomAuditEventRepositoryIntTest.addAuditEvent + addAuditEventTruncateLargeData. Root: JDK 9+ Instant/Clock carries sub-millisecond precision that the H2 round-trip truncates; the test asserts exact timestamp equality -> org.junit.ComparisonFailure 'expected <...46.629[129]Z> but was <...46.629[]Z>'. Brittle test, not a happy-path migration gap. This exact pair is also the residual 2 left after the Mockito fix on sha f6e0de65 (the other jaweb case), confirming it is independent of the Mockito/JDK11 issue.

MOCKITO_SHADED_BB / FIXABLE (verified remedy). Deterministic bump_8_to_11 repro: bump_rc=0, pre_pass=199 -> post_pass=10 (185 lost). Cluster label 'OOM' is WRONG -- OOM is downstream. Real root (817x): old Mockito's shaded ByteBuddy calls sun.misc.Unsafe.defineClass, REMOVED in JDK 11 -> UnsupportedOperationException 'Cannot define class using reflection' / NoSuchMethodException sun.misc.Unsafe.defineClass -> MockitoException -> every @MockBean IntTest context fails -> repeated failed context loads leak heap -> OutOfMemoryError as the tail symptom. mockito-core 2.15.0 is pinned by the jhipster-dependencies:2.1.1 BOM import (NOT spring-boot-parent), so a <mockito.version> property is a NO-OP and a byte-buddy dM override is moot (Mockito shades BB). FIX (verified): insert an explicit <dependencyManagement> mockito-core 2.23.4 (+ objenesis 3.2) BEFORE the BOM import -> post_pass 14 -> 197/199. Residual 2 = the audit-timestamp tests (see the other sha). Candidate java11_compat dependency-floor (Mockito), same family as the shipped surefire floor.

EE deps -> real <dependencies> (dependencyManagement bug); committed fa642329

OLD_VALIDATOR_JDK11 (root cause corrected; the prior SOURCE_MIGRATION/Mockito-1.x label is STALE). Deterministic bump_8_to_11: bump_rc=0, recipe [java8_to_java11] OK, surefire floor FIRED, and the Mockito floor correctly SKIPPED (Mockito-1.x era, outside the 2.x guard) -> the shipped Mockito floor is verified SAFE on Mockito-1.x source. Baseline green (6 pass). 3 FreemarkerTemplateServiceTest @IntegrationTest tests regress: ozimov is a Spring Boot 1.x app whose bundled Hibernate Validator 5.x parses java.specification.version expecting the legacy 1.8 shape (split on dot, read index 1). On JDK11 that property is the single token 11 (length 1) -> ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException 'Index 1 out of bounds for length 1' -> javax.validation 'Unable to instantiate Configuration' -> Failed to load ApplicationContext. Third member of the version-parse cluster (after Jadira + maven-jar-plugin). Fixable in principle by bumping hibernate-validator to 6.x, but SB1.x is Bean Validation 1.1 and HV6 is 2.0 (API risk); the SKILL bean-validation row does NOT trigger (it keys on 'no provider found', whereas here the provider is present but crashes). Niche SB1.x repo.

CONSERVED_FALSEPOS (prior SOURCE_MIGRATION/assertion-drift label is WRONG). Deterministic bump_11_to_17: the lombok_safe_bump step fails (bump_rc=1) -- `mvn rewrite:run` regenerates the assertj-assertions-generator SoftAssertions and javac rejects an erasure name clash (assertThat(ArchitectureRulesViolatedTest) vs inherited assertThat(LongAdder) from StandardSoftAssertionsProvider). BUT the bump aborts before changing anything and the project is already JDK17-compatible, so test_post on the unmodified tree under JDK17 CONSERVES all 95 baseline tests (rc_post=0, pre=95==post=95, lost=0) -> reward met (compiles under jv_to + BASELINE_PASS subset of post). The rung-2 FAIL_test_conservation was the agent hitting the (non-table) lombok-step assertj clash and not recovering, not a real regression. Latent bump-script robustness note: lombok_safe_bump (rewrite:run) can fail on assertj-assertions-generator projects; harmless here (no migration needed), would matter only for an assertj-generator project that also genuinely needs the bump. Niche; not chased.
